title: Perularia ussuriensis
taxonomy: Perularia ussuriensis is classified in the family Annonaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as custard apples or soursops. This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Perularia ussuriensis being part of the subfamily Annonoideae. The exact classification of Perularia ussuriensis may be subject to change as new research emerges, but currently, it is placed within the genus Perularia, which is a small group of flowering plants found in East Asia. The classification of Perularia ussuriensis is based on a combination of morphological and molecular studies.

intro: Perularia ussuriensis is a plant species that belongs to the family Berberidaceae, commonly known as the barberry family. It has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Perularia ussuriensis include alkaloids, such as berberine and palmatine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
